channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: PUBG Beta Testing New AI-Focused ‘Ally Duo’ Game Mode, Dates Revealed
item.content:encoded:
One of the OG battle royale shooters is about to broaden its horizons even further, as PlayerUnknown’s Battlegrounds, AKA PUBG, is allowing players to test out the new Ally Duo mode, which sees you battle in various scenarios and situations.

Can you believe that PUBG is nearly 10 years old? The battle royale phenomenon is still kicking, and hundreds of thousands of players still hop on daily. Krafton and PUBG Studios are keeping the shooter alive and well, and it’s time to welcome Ally Duo to the fold.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

PUBG Ally Duo Beta Test Runs From June 17 to July 1

This is being built up as an evolution of PUBG. The publisher has said, ‘PUBG Ally uses speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) technologies to interact with players in real time,’ and it’s believed this can create a new experience never before seen or played.

The beta takes place from available from 17 June to 1 July, so you’ve got plenty of time to see if this breakthrough is for you.

  • More Insider Gaming News: Hellraiser: Revival Release Date Revealed in New Trailer

A press release from Krafton explained to us:

‘Ally Duo is a new Arcade mode in which players form a two-player team with the AI character, Ella, and play on the Sanhok map. Players can communicate with Ella via voice interaction to collaborate across a range of in-game situations, including movement, item collection, and strategy. This mode allows players to team up with an AI companion, creating a uniquely immersive, cooperative gameplay experience.

Ella is the companion character built on PUBG Ally, KRAFTON’s AI technology. PUBG Ally is a new type of CPC (Co-Playable Character) designed to play alongside human players. Powered by an on-device small language model built on NVIDIA ACE, it is designed to understand players’ voice commands and in-game situations to respond proactively.’

Could this pave the way for a new future of AI-influenced game modes that conventional playlist options simply can’t offer? People are sure to have opinions about this.

Learn even more about the innovative game mode below.

Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord if you’re considering trying PUBG’s latest craze!

Check out TBH: Task Bar Hero challenging Counter-Strike 2’s dominance on Steam for more news. For more Insider Gaming content delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Hellraiser: Revival Release Date Revealed Alongside Horrifying New Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival has been given an official release date by Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games. The announcement landed today alongside a visceral, horrifying, and downright gratuitous trailer that is reserved exclusively for the strong of heart and mind.

This promises to be one of the most violent games of the year, and the trailer is so graphic that I can’t even embed it below.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Hellraiser: Revival Coming In Time for Halloween

Hellraiser: Revival is headed to XBOX, PlayStation 5, and PC platforms on October 8, 2026. That means it’ll be one of Halloween’s must-buy horror games, and judging from what’s coming, those with a penchant for gory titles will fall in love with this game.

When Hellraiser: Revival launches, it’ll come with two editions: standard and deluxe. Pre-order bonuses are being lined up in the form of cosmetics, and those with a love for all this horror can invest a little deeper to secure the coveted collector’s edition.

In terms of pricing, the standard edition will carry an amicable price tag of just $39.99, while the deluxe edition will set you back $49.99.

The collector’s edition, which has three tiers, will run you up a heftier bill.

The lowest tier of the collector’s edition, the Genesis Edition, has a $129.99 price tag, but the top-shelf version will cost $249.99. This ultimate tier, the Signed Pinhead Edition, comes with a huge amount of additional content and items, and it’s limited to just 1,500 copies.

Here’s the release date trailer, but it’s the slightly censored, toned-down version and not the red band version, which includes full frontal nudity and extreme violence and gore:

Are you looking forward to Hellraiser: Revival, you sick puppy? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders has (kind of) a new PvE map condition

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: “I just don’t give a f**k about anything” – w0xic on Aurora Roster Rumors
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to legendary Turkish AWPer Özgür ‘w0xic’ Eker ahead of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major Playoffs. w0xic speaks about Aurora’s Major run, the roster rumors swirling around the team, and his part in the iconic 2019 mousesports roster.

How does it feel to going to the Lanxess?

It’s good. I mean, the major playoffs are always good and giving you exciting vibes that if you can reach the quarters, you can reach the final too.

So I’m happy overall that we made the playoffs in the biggest tournament in the world.

Do you feel like you got an easy run to the playoffs?

Yeah, I could tell. I think we played only Spirit, who are a higher level than others. But I think 9z too was great, but I think we did a good job on that game.

I think one of the up-and-coming tier one teams to be scared of in the scene.

But yeah, overall, we didn’t face Vitality, we didn’t face NAVI, and the rest of the boys, like Mouz, so this is how it is.

Sometimes, sometimes it’s this way, and we were always playing very tough opponents every time, game after game, and it became something like this is because it depends on the other team’s results.

What do you think has changed at this event, because you look more relaxed?

We got used to it because we have been playing together for enough time, but soulfly just came as a new member. He’s been here for five, six months.

So I think we just got used to each other as well with the team members, and also the atmosphere of the events and everything. And also the rest of the people, just giving everything to reach one more step further, because there is nothing to lose.

You know, we have been reaching the quarter, reaching the final, but we were always losing it in the end.

Only one tournament that we were able to win and the rest, it was just kind of an achievement and at the same time a failure for us. So I think we just said we’ll do our best, and that’s it. If it goes nowhere, it doesn’t matter. We’ll do our best.

You guys have been working with a mental coach. What kind of lessons do you think you’ve taken from that?

Personally, I have been already working in my previous teams as well with a mental performance coach. And I think it’s always good to have someone positive around you, always. Also, understanding the human being and everything, as a mindset.

Everyone is taking something different from that person, I think. I’m happy to work with it because there is always one face that always you can share the problems and you can talk privately. And also you can get positive vibes always from that person.

But I don’t know for the others what they are taking because it’s like confidential things.

So it’s more individual?

More individual, yes. This is more of an individual thing.

In terms of the team, there have been rumors about Aurora changing players. How have you kind of dealt with that?

I just don’t give a fuck about anything. I’m just trying to do my job.

It doesn’t matter I’m here or somewhere else. I need to give my everything to show, and also satisfy myself, and show the people as well that I can do anything in any pressure. It doesn’t matter.

It’s like I’m in or out. It’s not an important thing. This is now; I’m here. I’m just mainly focusing on this one.

If I’m there in next, next one, I’ll focus on next one. So I’m going to talk a bit about you individually.

Obviously, you had that amazing clutch the other day against 9z. I feel like maybe this year you’ve been given a bit more freedom to be more expressive. Do you think that’s true?

Personally, I don’t think that there is a special thing. It’s just what you have inside, and it just comes out with the individual work and teamwork, and also finding your spot inside the team and position-wise and everything.

I think that can give you a very great performance, but also make you down because you are trying to do something for your team.

So, sometimes everything is going right, and you can have like successful frags, clutches, and everything. So in the 9z game, everything was kind of right for our own way, and also my way.

You’re facing BetBoom first. How would you assess their strengths?

They are really playing great lately. So, as people know, they qualified as well for the playoffs, they are a kind of tough opponent.

They did a great job with Boombl4 coming back, and I think they’re playing good as a team. Also, tactically. There are experienced players, well, not some of them that much. But I think somehow they are really good, like 9Z.

Okay. And in terms of, I just want to ask you about, you know, obviously, you know, you’ve had an amazing career, you know, and I’m sure that right now you’re looking forward to this event and stuff like that. But like, one of the things that kind of constantly comes up in my kind of social media feed is people talking about that 2019 mouse, you know, roster.

More info here: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Playoff Preview

Yeah. Like, is that something like, do you feel like, like in retrospect that that was a special roster, like a special time?

I think that was a special roster, and it was a special time for all the players, for ropz, for frozen, for me. We were so young and upcoming rising stars in the scene.

I think it’s all of them is combined well under the Karrigan wings. He was one of the best in-game leaders in the world.

So, I think that was a kind of special thing for me to be witnessed and also the rest of the guys as well. So that’s why it was, it was good times.

How do you think it helped you develop as a player?

It’s, yeah, you’re learning too much because you are attending so many tournaments. You’re playing really good players next to you, and you are just attending winning events.

Of course, you’re learning the pressure, learning how to play in front of people. Also learning how the team things are working.

You know, inside the car, there are like many things. If some of them are not there, the car is not working. So every piece is how it should be standing. That’s what you’re learning it basically.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord!


For other news, see the League of Legends MSI play-ins bracket.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/w0xic-iem-cologne-2026-major-interview/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Moon Games CEO Says XBOX Leadership Was ‘Too Focused on Past’
item.content:encoded:
Moon Games Studio CEO Thomas Mahler has stated that XBOX leadership was “too focused on the past,” responding on social media to several reported closures by XBOX Game Studios.

Ninja Theory, the studio behind Hellblade, is being closed just days after revealing a new title at the XBOX Games Showcase, while Double Fine and other studios are seeking deals with XBOX to avoid closure.

The closures come amid sweeping changes from new XBOX CEO Asha Sharma, but Mahler believes that all the turmoil will ultimately be positive for the company.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link
  • iOS Link

Mahler Says XBOX Leadership ‘Too Focused on The Past’

In a lengthy post on X, Mahler explained how the ‘heartbreaking’ changes could be good for games and gamers, as XBOX “has struggled for a long time to identify, empower, and protect the key creative people and teams who could have kept the brand at the top.”

He claims that during the development of Ori and the Blind Forest, and its sequel, Will of the Wisps, it was “clear that the main focus was still Halo, Gears [of War], and Forza.”

  • More Insider Gaming News: Embark Makes Long-Awaited Free Loadout Change to ARC Raiders

Mahler had hoped that Microsoft would “turn Ori into their Mickey Mouse or Mario-esque mascot,” claiming he “emailed Phil [Spencer] plenty of times that XBOX should have their eyes on delivering magical experiences for kids and families as well, like Disney and Nintendo always have.”

However, he says, “the powers to be were probably too focused on the past for that to happen.”

XBOX still has a big year ahead, with the releases of Halo: Campaign Evolved and Gears of War: E-Day on the horizon, fresh off the back of Forza Horizon 6, and further changes are expected in the months ahead.

Do you agree with Thomas Mahler’s views on XBOX’s approach? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at Double Fine addressing the XBOX shutdown situation.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/moon-games-ceo-xbox-leadership-focused-on-past/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Embark Makes Long-Awaited Free Loadout Change to ARC Raiders
item.content:encoded:
If you’re any kind of ARC Raiders fan, you’ll know the situation with free loadouts, a mechanic in the game that allows users to enter a raid with a randomized kit. It’s a point of contention, as it has almost no downsides for the user taking the kit, but plenty of concerns for those who meet said player.

For the longest time, players more geared towards taking in ‘paid kits’ and running into other players to fight them have requested Embark Studios examine how they manage this mechanic, and now, months after launch, that has finally happened.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Free Loadouts Being Removed (Kind of) From ARC Raiders

In the latest ARC Raiders update, Embark Studios revealed it’s testing the removal of free loadouts, but only under certain conditions.

Those conditions are map conditions, and for the next three weeks, anyone loading into the Night Raid or Close Scrutiny conditions will find that they can only do so if they’re running a paid kit. There will be no free kit functionality on these map conditions for the next three weeks.

This is a test-bed event, with Embark Studios assessing how players react to this action before presumably rolling it out across other map conditions and criteria.

In an ideal world (my world, I guess), you’d have no free loadouts on any map condition, leaving only the players with paid kits and a bit of effort in their build to saunter into these more challenging environments.

  • RELATED: ARC Raiders PvE Map Condition Revealed

The team wrote in the patch notes for the 1.33.0 update for ARC Raiders:

We are disabling free loadouts for Night Raid and Close Scrutiny to increase the barrier of entry so that players commit to appropriate risk and reward in these scenarios that have better loot.

This is a time limited change that we will be testing for the next three weeks. We may add this limitation to more map conditions in the future after we’ve observed its impact and analyzed your feedback.

Do you think this change is a huge win? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Assassin’s Creed Shadows’ final update has been revealed and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: When Does Pokémon Champions Mobile Release? Exact Start Time & Date
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions is entering a new era with the long-awaited mobile launch of the title, bringing a flood of new players into the game, and a major update coincides with the drop.

Released earlier this year on Nintendo Switch devices, Pokémon Champions has become the format for Pokémon’s competitive VGC scene, and the doors are now set to open for those on mobile.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link
  • iOS Link

When Does Pokémon Champions Release on Mobile?

The release of Pokémon Champions on mobile is set for Wednesday, June 17, but time zone differences mean the exact date does vary.

For those in North and South America, Pokémon Champions releases on mobile in the late evening on June 16, while those in Europe will see the drop in the early hours of the morning on June 17.

  • More Pokémon: Pokémon Moving North American International Championship to Chicago From 2027

In the eastern part of the world, Pokémon Champions’ mobile release will vary from market to market, and the drop comes alongside a new update to the title on all devices.

Alongside the Pokémon Champions mobile release, the game shifts to Regulation M-B, the first change in ruleset since the title first released, coming fresh off the back of the North American International Championship at the weekend.

What Time Does Pokémon Champions Mobile Release?

Pokémon Champions’ mobile debut has a simultaneous release worldwide, becoming available across the globe at exactly the same moment.

This is instead of a staggered release, like many game releases, which sees titles drop in the likes of New Zealand and Australia first. However, such an approach doesn’t work for a major live title like Pokémon Champions.

  • More Pokémon: Pokémon Champions Mobile and Mega Raichu Release Confirmed

The exact timing varies significantly, with some set for a late night if they want to jump into Pokémon Champions on mobile, or experience the new update immediately, while others see the drop happen around lunchtime.

Take a look at the exact Pokémon Champions Mobile release date across all major time zones below.

Are you excited by the release of Pokémon Champions on mobile? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, check out how to get Jynxzi’s Rocket League tournament drops and cosmetics in the new LTE.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pokemon-champions-mobile-release-date-time/

item.title: TBH: Task Bar Hero is Challenging Counter-Strike 2’s Dominance on Steam
item.content:encoded:
A new Steam title has emerged from nowhere and is averaging around half a million concurrent players on Steam. Whether it can be classified as a game is one thing, but it has carried on an interesting Trend in recent years.

Steam Charts is usually propped up by the same few games: Counter-Strike 2, Dota 2, PUBG, etc. A new download has appeared in the form of TBH: Task Bar Hero, and even though it hasn’t been around long, it’s grabbing the attention of many.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Counter-Strike 2 Looking Over Its Shoulder At Task Bar Hero

The last time I checked, TBH: Task Bar Hero had a 24-hour peak of 524,715 concurrent players.

So, what is it exactly? It’s yet another idle title that basically plays itself in the background while you get on with your everyday life.

The enchanting charm about this one is that you basically have a pixelated performance art of RPG heaven going on whilst you’re sinking your hours into spreadsheets and social media.

  • More Insider Gaming News: Double Fine Addresses Shut Down Situation with Simple Message

It’s not the first idle game to crack the upper echelons of Steam Charts. Other notable examples are Bongo Cat, which reached an all-time peak of nearly 200K, whereas Banana was touching on 900K!

It means Task Bar Hero still has a ways to go to take the ultimate crown, but expect to see it near the top of Steam Charts for a while.

Have you downloaded TBH: Task Bar Hero, or are you considering giving it a go? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Take-Two’s CEO revealing the secrets behind getting rich. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
